warsaw.name: Warsaw, Poland
warsaw.description: Starting point in the Pale of Settlement. The pogroms are worsening — it is time to leave.
warsaw.historicalFact: Warsaw's Jewish Quarter housed over 300,000 Jews in the 1890s, the largest urban Jewish community in Europe.

krakow.name: Kraków, Poland
krakow.description: Ancient Polish city with a vibrant Jewish quarter — Kazimierz.
krakow.historicalFact: Kazimierz, Kraków's Jewish quarter, was home to some of Europe's most beautiful synagogues, dating back to the 15th century.

vienna.name: Vienna, Austria
vienna.description: Capital of the Austro-Hungarian Empire and hub of the Zionist movement.
vienna.historicalFact: Theodor Herzl published 'Der Judenstaat' in Vienna in 1896, igniting the modern Zionist movement.

trieste.name: Trieste Port, Italy
trieste.description: Adriatic port city — gateway to the Mediterranean and the sea voyage ahead.
trieste.historicalFact: Trieste was the primary embarkation point for Jewish emigrants heading to Palestine in the late 19th century.

mediterranean.name: Mediterranean Sea
mediterranean.description: The great crossing — weeks at sea on a crowded steamship toward the Holy Land.
mediterranean.historicalFact: The sea voyage from Trieste to Jaffa typically took 10-14 days on a steamship in the 1890s.

jaffa.name: Jaffa Port, Palestine
jaffa.description: Ancient port city — your first steps in the Holy Land, under Ottoman rule.
jaffa.historicalFact: Jaffa was the main point of entry to Palestine. Immigrants were often turned away by Ottoman authorities.

tel_aviv.name: Tel Aviv Settlement
tel_aviv.description: A new Jewish neighborhood north of Jaffa — sand dunes being transformed into a city.
tel_aviv.historicalFact: Tel Aviv was founded in 1909 as a Jewish neighborhood outside Jaffa, starting with just 66 families drawing lots for plots of sand.

rishon_lezion.name: Rishon LeZion
rishon_lezion.description: 'First to Zion' — one of the earliest Jewish agricultural settlements, famous for its vineyards.
rishon_lezion.historicalFact: Rishon LeZion was founded in 1882 by First Aliyah immigrants. Baron Rothschild funded its famous winery.

rehovot.name: Rehovot
rehovot.description: Agricultural settlement fighting malaria and cultivating citrus groves in the coastal plain.
rehovot.historicalFact: Rehovot was founded in 1890 and was notable for refusing Baron Rothschild's patronage, insisting on self-reliance.

hebron.name: Hebron
hebron.description: One of the oldest cities in the world — sacred to Jews, Christians, and Muslims alike.
hebron.historicalFact: Hebron's Jewish community is one of the oldest in the world, centered around the Cave of the Patriarchs — burial place of Abraham.

bethlehem.name: Bethlehem
bethlehem.description: Ancient hillside town with olive groves and stone terraces, just south of Jerusalem.
bethlehem.historicalFact: Bethlehem's Jewish community dates back millennia. Rachel's Tomb on the road to Bethlehem is one of Judaism's holiest sites.

jerusalem.name: Jerusalem
jerusalem.description: The Holy City — your destination. Three thousand years of history await.
jerusalem.historicalFact: By the 1890s, Jews were already the majority population in Jerusalem, though the Old City remained under Ottoman control.

CHILD.backstory: Mia's loving grandmother who keeps family traditions alive
CHILD.goal: Protect her granddaughter and preserve family heritage

specialAbility.name: Family Wisdom
specialAbility.description: Can predict weather and dangers, preventing disasters
specialAbility.drawback: Frail health, more susceptible to illness

CHILD.backstory: Mia's grandfather, skilled in old country ways
CHILD.goal: Use his knowledge to help the family survive

specialAbility.name: Old Country Knowledge
specialAbility.description: Knows traditional remedies and survival techniques
specialAbility.drawback: Moves slowly, slows down travel pace

CHILD.backstory: Mia's father, will do anything to keep his family safe
CHILD.goal: Ensure his daughter reaches the Holy Land safely

specialAbility.name: Protective Instinct
specialAbility.description: Takes damage for family members, preventing their injury
specialAbility.drawback: Suffers extra health loss when protecting others

CHILD.backstory: Mia's mother, keeps the family together through love
CHILD.goal: Maintain family unity during the hardships ahead

specialAbility.name: Family Unity
specialAbility.description: Prevents family members from leaving the group
specialAbility.drawback: Takes emotional damage when family is hurt

CHILD.backstory: Friendly neighbor who knows everyone in the community
CHILD.goal: Use her connections to help during the journey

specialAbility.name: Community Connections
specialAbility.description: Finds help and assistance in settlements
specialAbility.drawback: May leave if conditions become too harsh

CHILD.backstory: Neighbor known for finding things when needed most
CHILD.goal: Use his skills to gather resources for survival

specialAbility.name: Resourceful Scrounger
specialAbility.description: Finds extra supplies and food during events
specialAbility.drawback: Sometimes gets into trouble with his schemes

templates.goal: Build homes in the new settlement

templates.backstory: A young Zionist activist who organized secret meetings in her basement
templates.goal: Help build the first Hebrew-speaking community

departure.title: Farewell to Warsaw
departure.text: You say goodbye to family and friends. The streets of the Jewish Quarter grow small behind you. There is no turning back.

krakow_arrival.title: The Synagogues of Kazimierz
krakow_arrival.text: In Kraków's Jewish quarter, you find a thriving community. Local Jews offer advice and supplies for the journey ahead.

train_journey.title: Train Through the Empire
train_journey.text: The rattling train carries you through the Austro-Hungarian countryside. Fellow passengers share stories of those who've already made Aliyah.

herzl_encounter.title: The Spirit of Herzl
herzl_encounter.text: In a Viennese café, you read a pamphlet by Theodor Herzl: 'If you will it, it is no dream.' The words fill you with renewed purpose.

sea_voyage_begins.title: Setting Sail
sea_voyage_begins.text: You board a crowded steamship. The Adriatic gleams ahead. Passengers pray, cry, and sing as Europe fades from view.

storm_at_sea.title: Storm at Sea!
storm_at_sea.text: A fierce Mediterranean storm rocks the ship. Waves crash over the deck. Passengers are terrified — some fall ill.

choices.text: Help the crew on deck (risky but heroic)
Use medicine to help the sick

seasickness.title: Seasickness Spreads
seasickness.text: The cramped conditions below deck breed illness. Several in your party look pale and weak.

first_glimpse.title: First Glimpse of Eretz Yisrael
first_glimpse.text: Land! The coast of Palestine appears on the horizon. Passengers weep, embrace, and recite Shehecheyanu. You have arrived.

customs.title: Ottoman Customs
customs.text: Ottoman officials inspect your documents. A money changer at the customs house converts your rubles to Ottoman lira — the only currency accepted in Palestine.

jaffa_arrival.title: The Old Port of Jaffa
jaffa_arrival.text: Jaffa is ancient and bustling — Arabic, Hebrew, and a dozen other languages fill the air. The smell of oranges mixes with sea salt.

tel_aviv_founding.title: A City from Sand
tel_aviv_founding.text: You walk among the sand dunes north of Jaffa where Jewish families are building a new neighborhood. They call it Tel Aviv — 'Hill of Spring.'

wine_harvest.title: The Vineyards of Rishon LeZion
wine_harvest.text: The settlers of Rishon LeZion invite you to help with the grape harvest. The wine cellars funded by Baron Rothschild are impressive.

malaria_outbreak.title: Malaria in the Swamps
malaria_outbreak.text: The coastal plain is plagued by mosquitoes and malaria. Several in your party develop fever and chills.

choices.text: Use medicine to treat the sick

cave_of_patriarchs.title: The Cave of the Patriarchs
cave_of_patriarchs.text: You visit the ancient Cave of Machpelah where Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob are buried. The weight of three thousand years presses upon you.

bethlehem_rest.title: Rest Near Rachel's Tomb
bethlehem_rest.text: You pause at Rachel's Tomb on the road to Jerusalem. Pilgrims from many faiths pass by. Jerusalem is only a day's walk away.

western_wall.title: The Western Wall
western_wall.text: You stand before the last remnant of the Holy Temple. Tears stream down your face as you press a prayer into the ancient stones.

settlement_founding.title: A New Beginning
settlement_founding.text: You have arrived. The land stretches before you — rocky, dry, but full of promise. This is where you will build a new life.
